L1.area: Audio source I/O and codec initialization logic

L2.description: A NULL pointer dereference vulnerability exists in an audio source initialization routine where a resampling context may fail to be created but is later used without a NULL check, leading to a denial of service when processing a crafted audio file.

L3.area: Audio source avcodec backend initialization and resampling setup
L3.description: A NULL pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the avcodec-based audio source backend where, during initialization, a resampling context may fail to be created but subsequent code uses it without verifying it was successfully allocated, leading to a denial of service when processing a crafted audio file.

ground_truth.reason: The avcodec audio source constructor does not check whether the resampling context was successfully created before proceeding, allowing a NULL pointer dereference when processing a crafted audio file.
